former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たたしんしや かわにしし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Tabuto-jinja}} [[JA:多太神社 (川西市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48757073}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tada Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:川西市の建築物]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}Hirano 2-20-21 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]

It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=October 29|Q2951|WD=Q2951|1=October 29}} (autumn festival) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Tada Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tada Shrine''' (ただじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hirano, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. To distinguish it from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}} (now {{ill|lt=Tada Shrine|Q3513683|WD=Q3513683|1=Tada Shrine}}), which became a shrine in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, locals call this shrine "Tabuto Jinja." === Enshrined deities === The following four pillars are enshrined. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} * Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === The founding era is unknown. According to 'History of Kawanishi City,' this area was formerly called "Kawabe District Ōmiwa-gō," and it is said that a ritual group from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}) migrated here and founded the shrine by enshrining their ancestor {{ill|lt=Ōtataneko|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|lt=Ōtataneko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōtataneko}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}. When the current enshrined deities were adopted is unknown, but tradition says they were invited from the deities of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} made {{ill|lt=Tada-no-shō|Q24859505|WD=Q24859505|1=Tada-no-shō}} his base. After the {{ill|lt=Minamoto clan|Q599881|WD=Q599881|1=Minamoto clan}} made Tada-no-shō their base, the shrine stood in the northeast direction from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tadain|1=UNKNOWN}}, their residence, and was treated as a guardian deity and called "Hirano Myōjin"; it was known by this name through early modern timesSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|WD=Q7689|1=1693}}), but Momoyama-style features can be seen in elements such as rear toad-leg struts and transoms of side lattice panels. Records also state it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}), so those old materials are believed to have been reused. ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Honden1.jpg|Honden ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Shaden.jpg|Full view of shrine buildings ファイル:Tabuto Jinja Hukurokuju.jpg|Hokusetsu Seven Gods of Fortune: Fukurokuju ファイル:多太社社号標石 DRtrcr8V4AANsQY.jpg|Tada-sha shrine name stone marker === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Hyogo)|Q5769424|WD=Q5769424|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nose Electric Railway|Q3743743|WD=Q3743743|1=Nose Electric Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Bibliography === * Kawanishi City Board of Education, explanatory signboard at the shrine front === External links === {{Commonscat|Tabuto-jinja}} *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6305034.html Tada Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/settu/bun/st060705-01.html |title=Tada Shrine |date=20070711163744}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]]
